executiveSummary.text: AdvancedAdvT Limited is a sub-£60M market cap AIM-listed software company operating in the digital transformation and membership management space. The FY26 trading update (year ending 28 February 2026) confirmed revenue of approximately £53 million and adjusted EBITDA of not less than £14.4 million — ahead of market consensus. Adjusted EBITDA margins exceeded 27%, underpinned by approximately 80% recurring revenue. A share buyback programme launched in March 2026 signals board confidence, with nearly two million shares now held in treasury. The company remains pre-profitable on a reported EPS basis (-0.08) and carries the governance and liquidity constraints typical of the micro-cap AIM peer group. At 165p with improving margins and an active buyback, the risk-reward is acceptable in the SPECULATIVE BUY range for investors with high risk tolerance and a multi-year horizon.

businessModel.text: AdvancedAdvT provides digital transformation software solutions with a focus on membership management, subscription billing, and customer engagement tools for B2B clients in the UK and Europe. Revenue is derived from SaaS subscriptions, implementation fees, and transaction processing. Key customers span UK local government, membership organisations, and not-for-profit sectors. The subscription model provides predictable recurring revenue, while implementation fees add near-term cash generation. The FY26 update confirmed approximately 80% recurring revenue as a proportion of total revenues — a composition that provides meaningful revenue visibility. The shift in revenue mix toward SaaS subscriptions is the primary margin leverage driver as cohort-based retention improves and customer LTV rises. The company serves a niche but defensible position in the membership management stack, though competitive pressure from larger SaaS platforms is a standing risk. Analyst coverage is limited and the stock has low visibility — a characteristic that keeps the discount to intrinsic value wider than it would be for a larger-cap peer.

recentCatalysts.text: Share buyback programme (March 2026): The board announced a share buyback and return of capital programme on 3 March 2026. The company has been actively repurchasing shares since — cumulative purchases totalled 1,971,000 shares held in treasury as of mid-April 2026, including a purchase of 15,000 shares at 165p on 14 April 2026. The buyback signals board-level confidence in intrinsic value and is directly accretive to remaining shareholders. Sustained purchase activity reduces the free-float voting pool, which could provide ongoing price support. FY26 trading update ahead of expectations (February 2026): Full-year results for the year ending 28 February 2026 confirmed revenue of approximately £53 million and adjusted EBITDA of not less than £14.4 million — both ahead of market consensus. Adjusted EBITDA margins exceeded 27%, underpinned by approximately 80% recurring revenue. M&A potential: With approximately 80% recurring revenue, a 27%+ EBITDA margin profile, and a niche position in the membership management stack, AdvancedAdvT is a logical consolidation target for a larger software consolidator. The low analyst visibility and limited broker coverage that deter some investors also make the private market value harder to arbitrage away, potentially keeping the company below fair value on public markets for longer.

bullCase.text: Recurring revenue continues to grow organically, LTV/CAC ratio improves materially, EBITDA margin expands toward 30%+ as cohort retention matures, a trade sale or strategic acquisition at a premium to the current market cap is announced, AIM-to-MAIN market transition is considered, new enterprise contracts in FY27 provide multi-year revenue visibility.

baseCase.text: Recurring revenue grows at a mid-single-digit pace, margins hold at 27-30%, EBITDA remains positive and cash-generative, the buyback programme continues and gradually reduces share count, stock re-rates modestly as FY27 results confirm the trading update trajectory. At ~£55M market cap, modest multiple expansion on ~£14M EBITDA implies an acceptable entry.

bearCase.text: Revenue growth stalls as enterprise sales cycles extend, competition in the membership management space intensifies, recurring revenue cohort churn rises, the buyback is suspended due to cash constraints, working capital pressures require an equity raise at a material discount to the current price.

keyRisks.detail: At approximately £55M market cap, daily traded volume is insufficient for larger institutional positions without material price impact. This is a structural discount factor for any public-market investor.

keyRisks.risk: Limited broker coverage and low visibility
keyRisks.detail: Minimal sell-side research coverage limits price discovery and investor awareness. The stock trades on thin information flow — a characteristic that creates opportunity but also risk of mispricing in either direction.
Despite positive adjusted EBITDA (£14.4M), reported EPS is negative (-0.08). Investors requiring earnings visibility through a traditional P/E lens will find this unsuitable.
AIM listing entails reduced disclosure and governance requirements relative to the MAIN Market, increasing information asymmetry for investors.

keyRisks.risk: Customer concentration in UK public sector
keyRisks.detail: Significant revenue exposure to local government and public sector budgets creates cyclical vulnerability to spending cuts, delayed payment cycles, and procurement delays.

whoShouldOwnIt.owners: Investors with high risk tolerance and a multi-year horizon comfortable with AIM liquidity constraints and sub-£60M market cap. Appropriate for investors seeking early-stage SaaS exposure with a demonstrably improving recurring revenue profile and an active shareholder return programme. M&A-aware investors who understand the premium a strategic buyer might pay versus the current public market discount.
whoShouldOwnIt.avoiders: Investors requiring daily liquidity, those with mandate restrictions on sub-£250M market cap holdings, or anyone seeking earnings visibility through a traditional P/E lens. Not appropriate for income-focused strategies given the absence of dividends. Growth-at-a-reasonable-price investors who need broker coverage and visible earnings consensus should look elsewhere.

recommendation.text: SPECULATIVE BUY at 165p. The FY26 trading update was materially better than prior estimates: £53M revenue, £14.4M+ EBITDA at >27% margins, and ~80% recurring revenue. The share buyback programme — with nearly two million shares in treasury — signals board confidence and is directly accretive to remaining shareholders. The low-visibility, low-coverage profile keeps the public market discount wide, which is precisely what makes the speculative case interesting.
recommendation.entryFramework: BUY below 140p on further weakness (provides margin of safety to base-case intrinsic value and improves the risk-reward on the M&A optionality). HOLD between 140p and 175p. Position size should reflect binary liquidity risk, the unproven scalability of enterprise sales execution, and the pre-profitability reality on a reported EPS basis.
